data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the project. It has an MSBuild inline task that generates a C# file (`GeneratedInfo.cs`) into the intermediate output directory before compilation. The `Program.cs` references `TestProject.Generated.BuildInfo` which should come from the generated file.

The issue is that while the target generates the file, it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild needs to know about the file via `@(Compile)` for it to be compiled.

Let me build the project to confirm the error.

arguments.description: Build the project to see the failure

toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-195d7182f4f0405a9550f5f6da8a40a9 && dotnet build 2>&1 | tail -20", "description": "Build the project to see the failure", "initial_wait": 30}

data.content: The file is generated successfully but never added to the `@(Compile)` item group, so the compiler doesn't know about it. The fix is to include the generated file as a compile item inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The file is generated (we can see "Generated code file: obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs"), but the compiler doesn't know about it because it's not added to the `Compile` item group.

The fix is to add an `<ItemGroup>` with a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the `GenerateSampleCode` target, so that the generated file gets included in compilation.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes `GeneratedInfo.cs` to the intermediate output directory but never adds it to the `@(Compile)` item group. MSBuild generates the file, then the compiler has no idea it exists.

**Fix:** Added a `<Compile Include="..." />` item inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```
